intro: I am applying for the Data Engineer position focused on Data Lake to AWS Lakehouse migration. My background aligns closely with large-scale ETL modernization, AWS-based data platforms, Spark/PySpark processing, and migration of legacy data workflows into scalable cloud environments.
body: At CITI, I developed Python/Pandas ETL pipelines ingesting telemetry from more than 6,000 endpoints, replacing legacy manual processing and improving scalability, reliability, and reporting readiness. I also built AWS-based data workflows using S3, Glue, Athena, and Redshift to support high-scale analytics and forecasting workloads.

My experience includes migrating workloads from on-prem Oracle environments into AWS-based processing platforms while validating data quality and reporting consistency across systems. I have worked extensively with structured telemetry datasets, Parquet-based storage, Spark/PySpark processing, and Hadoop ecosystem technologies including Hive/HDFS.

In addition, I have experience supporting operational migrations and collaborating with stakeholders to ensure reporting accuracy, successful hand-offs, and confidence in migrated datasets. My work has consistently focused on scalable ETL processing, optimization of large datasets, and maintaining integrity across enterprise reporting pipelines.
conclusion: I am interested in contributing these skills to your datastore migration initiatives and supporting the transition from legacy data lake environments to modern AWS-hosted lakehouse platforms.
